review_notes: Steps 2 and 3 (implement AnalyserPipeline in analyser.ts, expose getPipeline in player.ts) are already implemented in the working tree. The plan's 'existing_code' section describes the pre-implementation state, but the staged changes show createPipeline() and getPipeline() are already wired.
Step 1 (pipeline tests in analyser.test.ts) is still needed — the current test file imports createPipeline but has no describe block exercising it.
Step 4 (player-level pipeline tests) is still needed — player.test.ts has no getPipeline() coverage.

The mock AnalyserNode in player.test.ts has getByteFrequencyData/getByteTimeDomainData as no-op vi.fn() — step 4 tests will need to add fill behavior (like analyser.test.ts does) to verify buffers contain data after poll().
Pre-allocated buffer approach is correct and matches the implementation. No per-frame allocation concern.
Acceptance criteria mapping is accurate: mute via GainNode downstream of AnalyserNode means analysis continues while muted.
No new files needed — all changes extend existing modules. Clean.
suggested_adjustments: Update the 'existing_code' summary to reflect that steps 2 and 3 are already done, so the implementer starts at step 1 (tests) without redoing implementation work.

In step 4, note that player.test.ts setupMocks() needs getByteFrequencyData/getByteTimeDomainData to fill the passed array (currently they are bare vi.fn() with no side effect), otherwise poll() buffer assertions will fail.

acceptance_criteria_coverage.Audio source is connected to an Analyser: covered — existing connectSource test + createPipeline takes AnalyserNode
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Frequency data is readable per frame: covered — step 1 pipeline.poll() frequency buffer tests
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Time-domain data is readable per frame: covered — step 1 pipeline.poll() timeDomain buffer tests
acceptance_criteria_coverage.Mute state does not disable analysis: covered — step 4 muted-poll test + existing audio-graph-while-muted test
